name: Sam Lockhart
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary science • Observational research • Space science applications
bio: Sam Lockhart is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University and a recipient of the university’s Presidential Fellowship. His academic work focuses on planetary science and astronomy, with interests in observational research and space science applications. As a graduate researcher, he is involved in advancing scientific understanding through both theoretical and observational approaches, contributing to ongoing research in planetary environments and astronomical systems. Lockhart is also engaged in the broader academic community, participating in research initiatives and professional development opportunities that support his growth as an early-career scientist.
